scope.summary:
Bind the native Rust editor/notes layer to the existing /workspaces/:id/loom/* backend APIs. No backend changes are permitted; this MT is pure frontend wiring.

Goal: create a typed Rust client module at src/frontend/backend/loom.rs that exposes async functions for the Loom block, graph, tag, view, and edge routes used by native Loom navigation and editor panes. The diff shape is one new file plus a re-export from src/frontend/backend/mod.rs.

The backend HTTP surface (read from handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs) exposes a large route set. This MT covers the subset relevant to native editor and navigator panes:

Block CRUD:
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks                            -> create_loom_block
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id                  -> get_loom_block
  PATCH  /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id                  -> patch_loom_block
  DELETE /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id                  -> delete_loom_block
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/knowledge        -> get_loom_block_knowledge_bridge
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/transclusion     -> get_loom_block_transclusion
  PUT    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/pin-order        -> set_loom_block_pin_order
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/breadcrumbs      -> get_loom_block_breadcrumbs
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/backlinks        -> get_loom_block_backlinks
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/blocks/:block_id/unlinked-mentions -> scan_loom_block_unlinked_mentions

Daily journal:
  PUT    /workspaces/:ws/loom/journals/:journal_date            -> open_daily_journal

Folders:
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders                          -> list_loom_folders
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders                          -> create_loom_folder
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id               -> get_loom_folder
  PATCH  /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id               -> update_loom_folder
  DELETE /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id               -> delete_loom_folder
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id/blocks        -> list_loom_folder_blocks
  PUT    /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id/blocks/:block_id -> add_block_to_loom_folder
  DELETE /workspaces/:ws/loom/folders/:folder_id/blocks/:block_id -> remove_block_from_loom_folder

Tags:
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/tags                             -> list_loom_tag_hubs
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/tags/:tag_block_id               -> get_loom_tag_hub
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/tags/:tag_block_id/blocks        -> list_loom_blocks_for_tag

Edges:
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/edges                           -> create_loom_edge
  DELETE /workspaces/:ws/loom/edges/:edge_id                  -> delete_loom_edge

Graph:
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/graph/traverse                  -> traverse_loom_graph
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/graph/local                     -> local_loom_graph
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/graph/global                    -> global_loom_graph

Views and search:
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/views/:view_type                -> query_loom_view
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/search                          -> search_loom_blocks
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/search-v2                       -> loom_search_v2
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/quick-switcher/recents          -> list_quick_switcher_recents
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/quick-switcher/recents          -> record_quick_switcher_recent
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/visual-debug                    -> loom_visual_debug_snapshot

Block Collection Views (MT-262):
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/views/definitions               -> create_block_view
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/views/definitions/:block_id     -> get_block_view
  PATCH  /workspaces/:ws/loom/views/definitions/:block_id     -> update_block_view
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/views/definitions/:block_id/results -> query_block_view_results

Wiki projection (MT-184/241):
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki                            -> list_loom_wiki_pages
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki                            -> compile_loom_wiki_projection
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/bootstrap                  -> bootstrap_project_wiki
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/drift-check                -> project_wiki_drift_check
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/fanout                     -> project_wiki_fanout
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/:projection_id             -> get_loom_wiki_projection
  DELETE /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/:projection_id             -> delete_loom_wiki_projection
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/:projection_id/regenerate  -> regenerate_loom_wiki_projection
  GET    /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki/:projection_id/stale       -> loom_wiki_projection_stale

Markdown import:
  POST   /workspaces/:ws/loom/import/markdown                 -> import_markdown_to_loom

Exact files to create/edit:
  - CREATE: src/frontend/backend/loom.rs
  - EDIT:   src/frontend/backend/mod.rs (add `pub mod loom;`)

Step-by-step implementation notes:
  1. Define Rust structs for core types:
     - LoomBlock { block_id, workspace_id, content_type (LoomBlockContentType enum), title?, pinned, journal_date?, derived (LoomBlockDerived), asset_id?, document_id?, ... }
     - LoomEdge { edge_id, workspace_id, source_block_id, target_block_id, edge_type (LoomEdgeType enum), ... }
     - LoomFolder { folder_id, workspace_id, name, color?, sort_mode, parent_folder_id?, project_ref?, ... }
     - LoomTagHub { block, sub_tags, tagged_block_count, backlinks }
     - LoomGraphSearchResult and LoomSearchFilters for search responses
     - LoomBreadcrumbTrail, LoomBacklink, LoomUnlinkedMention
     - LoomKnowledgeBridge { entity_id, receipt_id, ... }
     - LoomTransclusionResponse { block_id, workspace_id, source_document_id?, source_doc_version?, content_json?, resolved, unresolved_reason? }
     - BlockViewDefinition, BlockViewResults for MT-262 views
  2. Define request types:
     - CreateLoomBlockRequest { block_id?, content_type, document_id?, asset_id?, title?, pinned?, journal_date? }
     - LoomBlockPatchRequest { title?, pinned?, favorite?, journal_date?, pin_order?, add_tags: Vec<String>, remove_tags: Vec<String> }
     - CreateLoomFolderRequest { name, parent_folder_id?, color?, sort_mode?, sort_order?, project_ref? }
     - LoomFolderUpdate (matches backend patch fields)
     - CreateLoomEdgeRequest { source_block_id, target_block_id, edge_type }
     - CompileWikiRequest { title, block_ids: Vec<String> }
     - ImportMarkdownRequest { title, markdown }
     - LoomSearchV2Request (POST body matching backend loom_search_v2 handler)
  3. Implement one async fn per route using the shared BackendClient (see MT-037 for the BackendClient pattern). Workspace_id is always a path parameter.
  4. The loom.rs module does NOT require identity headers (x-hsk-actor-*): the loom routes use WriteContext::human(None) server-side and do not enforce the actor-header contract. Do not add headers that the backend does not require.
  5. For the patch_loom_block handler: add_tags / remove_tags are Vec<String> tag hub block IDs. These are NOT edge IDs; the backend resolves them to edges internally.
  6. For search-v2 (POST /loom/search-v2): the request body mirrors LoomSearchFilters + optional pgvector query embedding. Use serde_json::Value for the embedding field to avoid coupling to the embedding dimension.
  7. AccessKit note: this MT creates backend client code only, no widgets. When a calling widget (e.g. block navigator, graph panel, tag filter) is built, each interactive element must receive a stable AccessKit author_id, role, and actions. Document this requirement in the module-level doc comment so the widget author sees it.
  8. Register the module in src/frontend/backend/mod.rs.
acceptance_criteria: A cargo test calling get_loom_block against a mock HTTP server returning a valid LoomBlock JSON returns a typed LoomBlock without panicking.
A cargo test calling loom_search_v2 with a mock returning a search result JSON returns a non-empty result list.
A cargo test calling get_loom_block_transclusion with resolved=false returns a typed LoomTransclusionResponse with resolved==false and unresolved_reason set.
All public async functions have a one-line doc comment citing the backend route.

LoomBlockContentType, LoomEdgeType enums compile without unknown variant panics for the types present in the backend storage definitions (note, document, asset, journal, tag, canvas_board, collection).
proof_targets: cargo build --package handshake_frontend 2>&1 | tail -5  ->  no errors
cargo test -p handshake_frontend loom 2>&1  ->  all tests pass
grep -n 'pub mod loom' src/frontend/backend/mod.rs  ->  line present
grep -n 'pub async fn get_loom_block' src/frontend/backend/loom.rs  ->  present
implementation_notes: The backend loom API does NOT require x-hsk identity headers on read paths (unlike knowledge_documents). Do not add unnecessary headers.
LoomBlockContentType variants to cover: Note, Asset, Journal, Tag, CanvasBoard, Collection (check loom.rs for the full set from storage).

The wiki projection endpoints return a ServedWikiPage with a staleness_verdict field attached (compile-time fail-closed on the backend). Represent staleness_verdict as serde_json::Value in the client to avoid tight coupling to the WikiStalenessVerdict enum.
For graph endpoints (traverse/local/global): use serde_json::Value for the graph result body initially; a typed graph model can be extracted in a later MT when the graph pane widget is built.
The quick-switcher recent POST body needs { block_id, workspace_id } at minimum; check backend for the full QuickSwitcherRecentInput shape.
Do not hold workspace state in this module. All functions accept workspace_id as a parameter.
Pagination parameters (limit/offset) are passed as query params; use Option<u32> for optional params with sane defaults documented in comments.
ports_from_react: app/src/components/BlockCollectionView.tsx - reference for block list rendering and the view_type query pattern (views/:view_type route).
app/src/lib/loom_search_query.ts - reference for search filter shape passed to /loom/search and /loom/search-v2.
app/src/lib/loom_search_open_target.ts - reference for what the search result target fields look like.
app/src/components/loom_canvas_visual_harness.tsx - reference for canvas board placement interaction pattern.
app/src/lib/tiptap/loom_transclusion_node.ts - reference for how transclusion block_id is embedded in a document node.

risks: LoomBlockContentType or LoomEdgeType enum variant mismatch between client and backend causes deserialization failure when new variants are added to the backend.
patch_loom_block add_tags/remove_tags treated as edge IDs instead of tag hub block IDs causes backend 404/validation errors silently.
Transclusion response with resolved=false treated as a network error instead of a typed unresolved state causes blank renders instead of 'unresolved' indicator.
wiki staleness_verdict tightly coupled to a Rust struct causes compile failure when backend verdict shape changes; using serde_json::Value prevents this.
Workspace_id path parameter URL-encoded incorrectly causes 404 from the backend (workspace IDs may contain hyphens but not slashes; still require URL encoding in path segments).
Graph endpoints returning large result sets can stall the UI thread if deserialized synchronously; JSON body can be very large for global graph.
Search-v2 POST with a malformed pgvector embedding field causes backend 400; client must not fabricate embeddings.
minimum_controls: LoomBlockContentType and LoomEdgeType use #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")] and #[serde(other)] or #[non_exhaustive] to tolerate unknown variants without panicking.
LoomTransclusionResponse resolved=false handled as a first-class Ok result, not mapped to an error variant.
wiki staleness_verdict field typed as serde_json::Value in client struct.
Graph result bodies typed as serde_json::Value initially; graph pane widgets consume them through typed extractors added per-widget.
Cargo tests cover: block CRUD roundtrip, tag patch with add_tags/remove_tags, transclusion unresolved case, search-v2 response parsing.
